intro: Health reference values come from large population studies (NHANES, WHO, CDC). They provide a framework for interpreting your results, but your personal context matters most. Maximum daily caffeine based on body weight. Values are updated to 2026 and reviewed regularly to keep you informed.
keyTakeaway: Formula: **FDA 400 mg maximum, or 6 mg per kg of body weight**. Direct calculation with your weight. Your personalized safe daily caffeine intake.

fields.label: Body Weight (kg)
fields.help: Your weight in kilograms. Weigh yourself without clothes on an empty stomach for accuracy.

explanation:
**Normal health values** come from large population studies (NHANES, WHO, CDC). They vary by age, sex, ethnicity, and region. They're guidelines; your personal medical history always takes priority.

## How It's Calculated

Maximum daily caffeine based on body weight.

This calculator applies the formula with your weight, checks for typical ranges, and alerts you to unusual values.

## Normal Reference Values

| Parameter | Normal Range (Adults) |
|---|---|
| Blood Pressure | <120/80 mmHg |
| Resting Heart Rate | 60–100 bpm |
| Body Temperature | 36.1–37.2°C |
| Blood Oxygen (SpO2) | 95–100% |
| Fasting Blood Glucose | 70–100 mg/dL |
| HbA1c | <5.7% |
| Total Cholesterol | <200 mg/dL |
| LDL Cholesterol | <100 mg/dL |
| HDL Cholesterol | >40 mg/dL (M) / >50 mg/dL (F) |

## Final Notes

This calculation is a guideline only. For critical decisions (financial, medical, legal), consult a qualified specialist. Values are current as of 2026 and reviewed periodically.

faq.q: How much caffeine is safe for adults?
faq.a: The FDA recommends 400 mg per day (about 4 cups of coffee) for healthy adults.
During pregnancy, limit caffeine to less than 200 mg per day (about 1 cup of coffee).

faq.q: Can children and teens consume caffeine?
faq.a: Children should avoid caffeine. Adolescents should limit intake to under 100 mg per day.

faq.q: What does 6 mg per kg mean?
faq.a: This is an alternative guideline for athletes—multiply your body weight in kg by 6 to find your safe dose.

faq.q: What are the signs of too much caffeine?
faq.a: Excessive caffeine can cause rapid heartbeat, anxiety, insomnia, and headaches.
Yes—taking 3–6 mg per kg of body weight 30–60 minutes before exercise can boost performance.
Green tea (~30 mg per cup), dark chocolate (10–30 mg), some medications, and energy drinks all contain caffeine.

faq.q: When should I see a doctor about caffeine intake?
faq.a: Consult a doctor if your values fall outside normal ranges, you have associated symptoms, or you have a family history of heart or metabolic disease.

sources.name: NIST — National Institute of Standards and Technology

dataUpdate.notes: Fixed formula, no external data updates required
